theme.titleSystem: Current: system theme. Click to switch to light.
theme.titleLight: Current: light theme. Click to switch to dark.
theme.titleDark: Current: dark theme. Click to switch to system.

login.title: Sign in to CyberStrikeAI
login.subtitle: Sign in with a platform account. The default administrator is admin.

dashboard.title: Dashboard
dashboard.ctaSub: Describe your target in chat, AI will assist with scanning and vulnerability analysis
dashboard.noFactsDesc: In project-bound chats, the agent records targets, findings, and attack chains
dashboard.alertCriticalReason_one: {{count}} open critical vulnerability — please review immediately
dashboard.alertCriticalReason_other: {{count}} open critical vulnerabilities — please review immediately
dashboard.alertFailedReason_one: Tool success rate is low ({{count}} failed call) — check MCP monitor
dashboard.alertFailedReason_other: Tool success rate is low ({{count}} failed calls) — check MCP monitor
dashboard.alertHitlReason_one: {{count}} HITL request pending — Agent is waiting for your decision
dashboard.alertHitlReason_other: {{count}} HITL requests pending — Agent is waiting for your decision
dashboard.alertMcpDownReason_one: {{count}} External MCP server is down — related tools are unavailable
dashboard.alertMcpDownReason_other: {{count}} External MCP servers are down — related tools are unavailable
dashboard.dataStale: Data may be stale — please refresh
dashboard.recoFixCriticalDesc: Critical-level vulnerabilities should be addressed first
dashboard.recoApproveHitlDesc: Agent needs your decision to proceed
dashboard.recoRestartMcpDesc: Related tools are unavailable until MCP recovers
dashboard.recoCheckMonitorDesc: View failed request details in MCP monitor
dashboard.recoSetupMcpDesc: Install MCP server before Agent can invoke specific capabilities
dashboard.recoStartScanDesc: Describe your target in chat, AI will help execute
dashboard.noVulnDesc: This list shows recent records; new results appear here when detection completes in chat

projects.title: Projects
projects.selectOrCreateHint: Projects share a cross-chat fact board; target, environment, auth and other facts are auto-injected in bound conversations.
projects.factToolbarHint: Index includes key and summary only (must include what + where + how to verify); put attack chain / POC in body, and reproduce via get_project_fact.
projects.graphToolbarHint: Graph arrows match stored fact links (source → target). Nodes are layered target→infra→finding→exploit. Dashed edges are tentative.
projects.graphEmpty: No graph data yet. Add links on finding/exploit facts (discovered_on → target/*) to build the path.
projects.graphEmptyStep2: Record findings/exploits with links between facts
projects.graphEmptyStep3: Use Connect mode or edit facts to add relationships
projects.factLinksHint: One per line: type: source_fact_key (source → this fact). Common types: discovered_on, depends_on, leads_to, enables, exploits. Saving replaces all links.
projects.factIncomingLinksHint: Derived from outgoing links on source facts. e.g. finding discovered_on → target/* appears as incoming on the target; edit the source fact's outgoing links.
projects.graphEdgeTypePrompt: Edge type (discovered_on / leads_to / depends_on / enables / exploits)
projects.graphEdgesHint: Arrow direction matches the database and edit modal (source → target). Click an edge to focus it.
projects.graphEdgeSynthetic: Auto-generated from fact link; edit the fact to remove
projects.graphVulnSidebarHint: Linked vulnerability node. Use the button below to open it in Vulnerability Management.
projects.promoteAttackChainTitle: Promote conversation attack chain to project facts
projects.confirmPromoteAttackChain: Promote this conversation's attack chain into the project? Facts and edges will be created or updated.
projects.boundConversationsHint: Conversations bound to this project; click to open
projects.noMatchingVulns: No matching vulnerabilities, try adjusting filters
projects.modalNewSubtitle: After creation, bind conversations to share fact board across chats
projects.chatSelectorButton: Share fact board across chats after binding a project
projects.factHintAttackSparse: Attack-chain fact: fill complete body (steps, HTTP/command, response evidence); avoid conclusion-only notes. You can insert the attack-chain template.
projects.factHintAttackReady: Attack-chain fact: body is used for audit reproduction, keep original request/response and step-by-step flow.
projects.factHintEnv: Environment fact: body should include evidence source; for findings/exploitation use finding|chain|exploit|poc category.
projects.confirmOverwriteBodyTemplate: Overwrite current body content with template?
projects.restoreTitle: Restore as tentative and re-index into board
projects.descriptionLengthHint: For team reference only — the Agent does not read this. Keep it brief (max 4000 chars). Put anything the Agent should know in the fact board.
projects.noMatchingFacts: No matching facts, try adjusting filters
projects.noFacts: No facts yet. Click Add fact or let Agent write facts automatically
projects.noBoundConversations: No bound conversations yet; select this project in chat to bind
projects.confirmUnbindConversation: Unbind this conversation from current project?
projects.factSparseWarn: This fact belongs to attack-chain/exploit category, but body lacks reproducible structure (steps, HTTP/command, request/response, etc.). Edit and complete it for audit reproduction.
projects.noVulnerabilitiesInProject: No vulnerabilities in this project yet. Create one first or let Agent record it.
projects.promptConversationIdForVulnCreate: Conversation ID is required to create vulnerability (can be source conversation):
projects.confirmRestoreFact: Restore fact {{factKey}}? It will re-enter board index with tentative status.
projects.noVulnerabilityRecords: No vulnerability records in this project
projects.noFactsForVulnerability: This vulnerability has no related facts yet. Link vulnerability or generate vulnerability draft from fact detail.
projects.promptChooseFactByIndex:
This vulnerability is linked to {{count}} facts. Enter index to view:
{{lines}}
projects.writePermissionDenied: This account is read-only and cannot create or edit projects
projects.scopeNoteAuthorizedWebOnly: Authorized for Web application layer testing only
projects.invalidScopeJson: Invalid scope JSON, please fix it first or click Format
projects.confirmArchiveProject: After archiving, this project is hidden from active list by default. Continue?
projects.confirmDeleteProject: Delete this project? Facts will be deleted and conversations unbound.
projects.confirmSaveSparseFact:
This fact is attack-chain/exploit related, but body does not contain reproducible structure (steps, HTTP/command, request/response).
Save anyway? It's recommended to insert attack-chain template and fill POC first.
projects.clearStaleProjectBindingFailed: Failed to clear stale project binding
projects.noProjectsClickCreate: No projects yet, click New project below
projects.basicInfoHint: Name and description are shown in project details
projects.settingsIntroHint: Configure project metadata and Agent authorization boundary; takes effect immediately for bound conversations after saving.
projects.pinFact: Pin fact (prioritize in list and blackboard index)
projects.scopeHint: JSON format for Agent authorization boundary and target assets
projects.scopeFootnote: Supports targets, exclude, notes and more. Empty means no scope limit.
projects.dangerZoneHint: Archived projects are hidden unless 'Show archived' is enabled; deletion removes all facts permanently.
projects.saveChangesHint: Click save to sync changes to server
projects.factModalSubtitle: Summary is indexed on board; body stores attack chain and POC for audit reproduction (separate from vulnerability records).

chat.filterProjectSearchFailed: Failed to load projects. Try again.
chat.inputPlaceholder: Enter target or command... (type @ to select tools | Shift+Enter newline, Enter send)
chat.waitingAttachmentsUpload: Waiting for attachments to finish uploading…
chat.attachmentsUploadIncomplete: Some attachments failed to upload. Remove the failed items or pick files again before sending.
chat.expandDetailLazyHint: Expand details (loads iteration details on click)
chat.liveTimelinePruned: Collapsed the first {{count}} live process details. View the full record page by page after the task completes.
chat.noProcessDetail: No process details (execution may be too fast or no detailed events)
chat.deleteTurnConfirm: Delete this entire turn (user message and assistant reply)? This cannot be undone. The next reply will use only the remaining messages; saved context snapshots will be cleared.
chat.emptyGroupConversations: This group has no conversations yet.
chat.deleteGroupConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete this group? Conversations in the group will not be deleted, but will be removed from the group.
chat.deleteConversationConfirm: Delete this conversation? Chat messages cannot be recovered, but recorded vulnerabilities will remain in the vulnerability library.
chat.viewAttackChainSelectConv: Please select a conversation to view attack chain
chat.viewAttackChainCurrentConv: View attack chain of current conversation
chat.systemReadyMessage: System is ready. Please enter your test requirements, and the system will automatically perform the corresponding security tests.
chat.streamNetworkErrorHint: Connection lost ({{detail}}). A long task may still be running on the server; check running tasks at the top or refresh this conversation later.
chat.streamEndedWithoutDone: The connection ended before a completion signal was received. The task may still be running on the server; check running tasks at the top or refresh this conversation.
chat.einoStreamErrorMessage: Streaming read failed; the system will retry or terminate according to policy.
chat.einoEmptyResponseContinueMessage: Session ended without captured assistant text; resuming from trace…
chat.iterationLimitReachedMessage: Maximum iteration count reached; automatic iteration has stopped.
chat.einoPendingOrphanedMessage: Detected {{count}} unclosed tool call(s); marked as failed and finalized automatically.
chat.copyFailedManual: Copy failed, please select and copy manually
chat.agentModeEinoSingleHint: Eino ChatModelAgent + Runner with MCP tools (/api/eino-agent)
chat.agentModeDeepHint: Eino DeepAgent for complex security testing, multi-stage task delegation, and synthesis
chat.agentModePlanExecuteHint: Plan → execute → replan (single executor with tools)
chat.agentModeSupervisorHint: Expert-routing scenarios: route across multiple specialist sub-agents via transfer
chat.agentModeSingleHint: Eino ADK single-agent for chat and tool use
chat.agentModeMultiHint: Eino prebuilt orchestration: Deep for complex tasks, Plan-Execute for structured loops, Supervisor for expert routing
chat.reasoningPanelHint: Only Eino single- and multi-agent requests use these; merged with defaults in Settings.
chat.hitlReviewerHint: Switch between human and Audit Agent anytime; rules and whitelist stay the same. You can pre-select even when HITL is off.
chat.hitlWhitelistPlaceholder: e.g. read_file, grep or one tool per line (merged with global allowlist in config)
chat.hitlWhitelistHint: Separate with commas or new lines; shown merged with the global allowlist in config.
chat.hitlApplyOkSync: HITL settings saved and synced to the server.
chat.hitlApplyOkWhitelistYaml: Tool whitelist merged into config.yaml and active. Mode and timeout still require selecting a conversation and clicking Apply to sync session settings to the server.

hitl.pageReviewerHint: Applies to the selected conversation. Without a conversation, saved to config.yaml as the global default for new chats. Takes effect immediately.
hitl.whitelistHint: One per line or comma-separated. Saved to config.yaml global whitelist and takes effect immediately (synced with chat sidebar).
hitl.strategyHint: Whitelisted tools skip approval. Other tools are judged by the model using this prompt when Audit Agent is selected.
hitl.strategyHintApproval: Whitelisted tools skip approval. In approval mode the Audit Agent only approves or rejects.
hitl.strategyHintReviewEdit: In review & edit mode the Audit Agent may narrow parameters via editedArguments before approve; reject if parameters cannot be safely adjusted.
hitl.logsEmptyHint: Records are created automatically when HITL approvals are approved or rejected.
hitl.retentionHint: Audit logs are kept for {{days}} days, then purged automatically.
hitl.batchDeleteConfirm: Delete the selected {{count}} audit log(s)? This cannot be undone.
hitl.clearAllConfirm: Clear all {{count}} audit log(s) matching the current filters? This cannot be undone.
hitl.clearAllConfirmNoFilter: No filters are set. This will clear all {{count}} audit log(s). This cannot be undone. Continue?
hitl.selectLogsFirst: Select audit logs to delete first
hitl.reviewEditHelp: Review & edit mode: provide a JSON object to override tool arguments. Example: {"command":"ls -la"}
hitl.approvalHelp: Approval mode: only approve/reject, argument editing is disabled.
hitl.commentHelp: Comment (optional): briefly note the approval reason.

progress.lastIterSummary: Last iteration: generating summary and next steps...
progress.analyzingRequestPlanning: Analyzing your request and planning test strategy...

tasks.title: Task Management
tasks.interruptModalHint: When a tool is running: same as MCP monitor "Stop tool" — only that call is stopped and the run continues; your note can be merged into the tool result (USER INTERRUPT NOTE). When no tool is running (model thinking/streaming only): "Interrupt & continue" still works — current output pauses, your note is merged into context and the run resumes automatically; the progress timeline shows a "User interrupt & continue" entry. Use this instead of a full stop when you only want to steer; use "Stop completely" to end the whole task.
tasks.interruptReasonPlaceholder: e.g. Tool is too slow—skip and summarize…
tasks.interruptReasonRequired: Please enter a short note so the model can continue accordingly.
tasks.historyHint: Tip: Completed task history available. Check "Show history" to view.
tasks.taskInfoNotSynced: Task info not synced yet, please try again later.
tasks.createBatchQueueFailed: Failed to create batch task queue
tasks.noBatchQueues: Currently there are no batch task queues
tasks.viewVulnerabilitiesQueueTitle: View vulnerabilities: open management filtered to this queue
tasks.confirmRunSingleTask: Run this task only? The queue will pause when it finishes and will not continue other pending items.
tasks.runSingleTaskUnavailable: Unavailable while the queue or a task is running
tasks.runSingleTaskUnavailableQueue: Queue is running; pause it before running another task individually
tasks.cronRoundDoneProgressHint: Cron queue: subtasks finished; next round starts on schedule
tasks.cronRunningProgressHint: This round is running; the next full cycle follows Cron / next run time
tasks.cronPendingProgressNote: Will start on schedule, or click Start to run a round now
tasks.cronRecurringCallout: Cron queues start a new round at each scheduled time. Turn off "Allow Cron auto-run" to stop looping.
tasks.pauseQueueConfirm: Pause this batch queue? The current task will be stopped; remaining tasks will stay pending.
tasks.deleteQueueConfirm: Delete this batch queue? This cannot be undone.
tasks.rerunQueueConfirm: Rerun this batch queue? All tasks will be reset to pending and re-executed.

webshell.title: WebShell Management
webshell.noConnections: No connections. Click "Add connection" to add one.
webshell.selectOrAdd: Select a connection from the list or add a new WebShell connection.
webshell.cmdParamPlaceholder: Leave empty for cmd; e.g. xxx for xxx=command
webshell.encodingHint: Switch to GBK or GB18030 if the Simplified Chinese Windows target shows garbled output.
webshell.osHint: Determines whether file manager / uploads use Linux or Windows commands. Choose Windows for PHP/JSP hosted on Windows.
webshell.dbNoConn: Please select a WebShell connection first
webshell.dbRunning: Database command is running, please wait
webshell.dbCliHint: If command not found appears, install mysql/psql/sqlite3/sqlcmd on the target host first
webshell.dbNoSchema: No schema yet, click Load Schema
webshell.dbSelectTableHint: Click a table to expand columns and generate SQL
webshell.aiSystemReadyMessage: System is ready. Please enter your test requirements, and the system will automatically perform the corresponding security tests.

webshell.aiMemoPlaceholder: Save key commands, testing ideas, and repro steps...

webshell.testNoExpectedOutput: Shell responded but expected output was not found. Check password and command parameter name.
webshell.waitFinish: Please wait for the current command to finish

mcp.alwaysVisibleHint: Always keep visible in Tool Search results
mcp.alwaysVisibleBuiltinHint: Backend builtin tool is pinned by default and cannot be disabled
mcp.loadToolsTimeout: Tools load timeout. External MCP may be slow. Click Refresh to retry or check connection.

mcp.configStdioNeedCommand: Config error: "{{name}}" stdio mode needs command
mcp.configHttpNeedUrl: Config error: "{{name}}" http mode needs url

mcp.connectionFailedCheck: Connection failed. Check config and network.
mcp.connectionTimeout: Connection timeout. Check config and network.

hitl.title: Human-in-the-loop
hitl.defaultReviewerHint: Used when no conversation is selected and for new conversations; the chat sidebar can still override it.
hitl.auditModelBaseUrlPlaceholder: Leave blank to reuse the main Base URL
hitl.auditModelApiKeyPlaceholder: Leave blank to reuse the main API Key
hitl.auditModelNamePlaceholder: Leave blank to reuse the main model; a small model is recommended
hitl.auditModelHint: Used only for Audit Agent approvals; manual approval does not call a model.
hitl.retentionDaysHint: 0 keeps logs forever; blank uses the 90-day default.
hitl.toolWhitelistHint: One per line or comma-separated; whitelisted tools skip human-in-the-loop approval.
hitl.auditPromptHint: Leave blank to use the backend default strategy.
hitl.auditPromptReviewEditHint: Review-edit mode can approve with narrowed editedArguments.

c2.title: C2
c2.enableLabel: Enable built-in C2 (listeners, sessions, payloads, MCP tools)
c2.enableHint: When off, listeners are not started and C2 MCP tools are not registered; the C2 sidebar is hidden—useful for local-only chat/knowledge deployments. Click Apply to save.

robots.title: Bot settings
robots.description: Configure WeChat (iLink), WeCom, DingTalk and Lark bots so you can chat with CyberStrikeAI on your phone without opening the web UI.
robots.managerDesc: Choose a bot type first, then configure only the fields that belong to that platform.
robots.emptyDesc: Pick a platform above, or use New bot to choose by type.
robots.createDesc: Choose a bot type to open its dedicated setup. This version supports one built-in configuration per platform.

wechat.title: WeChat / iLink
wechat.subtitle: Bind personal WeChat via QR code and chat with CyberStrikeAI on your phone
wechat.bindHint: Scan with WeChat to confirm; settings are saved automatically.

chatFilesPage.title: File Management
chatFilesPage.intro: Files uploaded in chat appear here. Drag files into the list below, or click Upload to pick files (multiple allowed). Click “Copy path” to copy the server absolute path and paste it into a conversation so the model can reference the file.
chatFilesPage.folderPathCopied: Folder path copied — paste into chat if needed
chatFilesPage.confirmDeleteFolder: Delete this folder and everything inside it? This cannot be undone.
chatFilesPage.folderRemovedStale: That folder is not on the server anymore; list refreshed.
chatFilesPage.mkdirExists: A file or folder with that name already exists
chatFilesPage.mkdirInvalidName: Invalid name: cannot be empty or contain /, \, or use . or ..
chatFilesPage.copyPathTitle: Copy the absolute path on the server; paste into chat to reference this file
chatFilesPage.pathCopied: Path copied — paste it into chat
chatFilesPage.uploadOkHint: Uploaded. Use “Copy path” to copy the absolute path.
chatFilesPage.moreActions: More: open chat, edit, rename, delete
chatFilesPage.editBinaryHint: Binary files (images, archives, etc.) cannot be edited as text here. Use Download and open locally.
chatFilesPage.editTooLarge: File exceeds 2MB and cannot be edited here. Download and edit locally.
chatFilesPage.errorGeneric: Something went wrong. Please try again.

tasksPage.searchQueuePlaceholder: Search queue ID, title or created time

agentsPage.title: Agent management
agentsPage.hint: Agents are .md files under agents_dir (front matter + body as system prompt). The orchestrator is the Deep coordinator and is not listed as a task sub-agent.
agentsPage.empty: No Markdown sub-agents yet. Click New agent to create one.
agentsPage.roleHint: Orchestrators are mode-specific: Deep → orchestrator.md (or one .md with kind: orchestrator); plan_execute → orchestrator-plan-execute.md; supervisor → orchestrator-supervisor.md. At most one of each. Empty body falls back to multi_agent.orchestrator_instruction / orchestrator_instruction_plan_execute / orchestrator_instruction_supervisor or built-in defaults (PE/SV do not reuse Deep orchestrator_instruction).
agentsPage.filenameInvalid: File name must end with .md and use only letters, digits, ._-
agentsPage.fieldId: Agent id (optional; derived from name if empty)
agentsPage.descPlaceholder: When the orchestrator should delegate to this agent
agentsPage.fieldTools: Tools (comma-separated; same keys as role tools)

settingsBasic.modelsListSuccess: Loaded {count} models — use the dropdown on the right, or type in the input
settingsBasic.modelsListClaudeHint: Claude does not support auto model list; enter the model name manually
settingsBasic.maxTotalTokensHint: Shared by memory compression and attack chain building. Default: 120000
settingsBasic.openaiReasoningHint: Applies to Eino single-agent and multi-agent only; works with chat-page reasoning controls.
settingsBasic.openaiReasoningAllowClient: Allow chat page to override reasoning options
settingsBasic.enableMultiAgentHint: After enabling, the chat page can use multi-agent mode; sub-agents are set in multi_agent.sub_agents or the agents/ directory. Orchestration is configured below.
settingsBasic.multiAgentOrchestrationHint: deep = DeepAgent + task; plan_execute = plan / execute / replan; supervisor = expert routing (transfer, specific scenarios). Takes effect after save & apply.
settingsBasic.multiAgentPeLoopHint: Only for plan_execute; max execute↔replan rounds.
settingsBasic.multiAgentRobotModeHint: Execution mode for WeCom / DingTalk / Lark bot messages. Deep / Plan-Execute / Supervisor require multi-agent to be enabled.
settingsBasic.userLedgerMaxRunes: User input ledger total character limit
settingsBasic.userLedgerMaxRunesHint: Total limit for the original user input ledger injected after compaction; 0 uses the backend default. DB-stored original messages are not truncated.
settingsBasic.userLedgerEntryMaxRunesHint: Model-visible limit for each user message in the ledger; 0 uses the backend default and does not affect DB originals.
settingsBasic.latestUserMaxRunesHint: Runtime limit for the current user message sent to the model; oversized input is persisted and only a preview is injected. 0 uses the backend default.
settingsBasic.latestUserHeadRunesHint: Head characters kept visible to the model for oversized current user input.
settingsBasic.latestUserTailRunesHint: Tail characters kept visible to the model for oversized current user input.
settingsBasic.multiAgentBatchUse: Use multi-agent for batch task queues
settingsBasic.multiAgentBatchUseHint: When enabled, each sub-task executed by queue in Task Management will run through Eino DeepAgent (requires multi-agent).
settingsBasic.embeddingBaseUrlPlaceholder: Leave empty to use OpenAI base_url
settingsBasic.embeddingApiKeyPlaceholder: Leave empty to use OpenAI api_key
settingsBasic.topKHint: Number of top-K results to return
settingsBasic.similarityHint: Results below this value are filtered (0-1)
settingsBasic.subIndexFilterPlaceholder: e.g. prod, must match an indexing sub_indexes tag
settingsBasic.subIndexFilterHint: Empty = no filter. When set, only rows whose sub_indexes contain this tag (legacy rows with empty sub_indexes still match).
settingsBasic.ragPipelineHint: MultiQuery and rerank are always on: LLM query rewrite → vector prefetch & fusion → HTTP rerank → dedupe & budget truncate.
settingsBasic.multiQueryMaxQueriesHint: Max LLM-generated retrieval variants (including paraphrases of the original query). Recommended 3–4, max 8.
settingsBasic.rerankProviderHint: DashScope uses gte-rerank; other compatible endpoints use /v1/rerank. Leave empty to infer from Base URL below.
settingsBasic.rerankBaseUrlPlaceholder: Leave empty to reuse embedding / OpenAI base_url
settingsBasic.rerankApiKeyPlaceholder: Leave empty to reuse embedding / OpenAI api_key
settingsBasic.rerankApiKeyHint: On rerank failure, results fall back to fusion order; search still works.
settingsBasic.postRetrieveDedupeAuto: Results are always deduped by normalized text (whitespace-collapsed bodies). No setting required.
settingsBasic.prefetchTopKHint: Vector candidates per MultiQuery variant; 0 uses built-in max(top_k×4, 20) (max 200).
settingsBasic.maxContextCharsHint: 0 = unlimited; keeps whole chunks in rank order until the budget is exceeded.
settingsBasic.maxContextTokensHint: 0 = unlimited; tiktoken estimate (embedding model name, fallback cl100k_base).
settingsBasic.chunkStrategyHint: Matches Eino-style pipelines: Markdown headers + recursive for docs; plain text can use recursive only.
settingsBasic.requestTimeoutHint: 0 uses the default 120s embedding HTTP client timeout.
settingsBasic.batchSizeHint: Max texts per embedding request (SQLite indexer batches writes accordingly).
settingsBasic.preferSourceFile: Prefer on-disk source file when indexing (Eino FileLoader)
settingsBasic.preferSourceFileHint: When enabled, content comes from file_path; falls back to DB content if load fails.
settingsBasic.subIndexesHint: Passed to indexer.WithSubIndexes; stored in the sub_indexes column.
settingsBasic.maxChunksHint: Max chunks per knowledge item (0 = no limit)
settingsBasic.maxRpmHint: Max requests per minute (0 = no limit)
settingsBasic.maxRetriesHint: Retries on rate limit or server error
settingsBasic.visionEnabledHint: Registers the MCP tool when enabled; images are sent only for one VL call; agent context keeps text summaries only. Save & apply to take effect.
settingsBasic.visionBaseUrlPlaceholder: Leave empty to reuse OpenAI Base URL
settingsBasic.visionApiKeyPlaceholder: Leave empty to reuse OpenAI API Key
settingsBasic.visionModelRequired: Vision model name is required when vision is enabled
settingsBasic.visionSkipPreprocessHint: 0 = always JPEG compress; must also fit long-edge and payload limits.
settingsBasic.visionTestFillRequired: Enter vision model and ensure API Key is available (or reuse OpenAI)
settingsBasic.testFillRequired: Please fill in API Key and Model first

settingsTerminal.title: Terminal
settingsTerminal.description: Run commands on the server for ops and debugging. Commands run on the server; avoid sensitive or destructive operations.
settingsTerminal.welcomeLine: CyberStrikeAI Terminal — real shell session; type commands directly. Ctrl+L to clear screen
settingsTerminal.xtermNotLoaded: xterm.js failed to load. Refresh the page or check your network.

settingsAudit.title: Audit logs
settingsAudit.description: Platform admin actions (login, config, deletes). Does not log chat content, per-command terminal/WebShell runs, or per-tool invocations.
settingsAudit.filterCascadeHint: Select a category to filter by action
settingsAudit.retentionHint: Audit records are kept for {{days}} days, then purged automatically.
settingsAudit.disabledHint: Audit logging is disabled; new actions are not written.
settingsAudit.filterTimeZone: Timezone: {{tz}} (filter uses your browser's local time)

auth.change_password_failed: Password change failed: current password incorrect

config.apply_fail_kb_init: Failed to apply config: knowledge base init
config.apply_fail_kb_reinit: Failed to apply config: knowledge base re-init

settingsSecurity.changePasswordDesc: After changing password, sign in again with the new password.

settingsRobotsExtra.botCommandsEntryDesc: View the Chinese and English commands available in robot chats, including common conversation, role, and project actions.
settingsRobotsExtra.botCommandsDesc: You can send the following commands in chat (Chinese and English supported):
settingsRobotsExtra.botCmdCurrent: Show current conversation, role and project
settingsRobotsExtra.botCmdNewProject: Create project and bind current conversation
settingsRobotsExtra.botCmdBindProject: Bind current conversation to a project
settingsRobotsExtra.botCommandsFooter: Otherwise, send any text for AI penetration testing / security analysis.

mcpDetailModal.title: Tool call details
mcpDetailModal.runningNoResponseYet: No output yet; the tool may still be running. If it hangs, use "Stop tool" below to end this call only.
mcpDetailModal.abortHint: Stops only this tool call. The conversation / multi-step task continues (unlike stopping the whole task).
mcpDetailModal.abortConfirm: Stop this tool call? The overall conversation or iterative task will not be cancelled.
mcpDetailModal.abortSuccess: Cancellation requested; status will update when the tool returns.
mcpDetailModal.abortNoteModalHint: Optional: why you stopped or how the model should continue. The model sees any tool output first, then a labeled block (USER INTERRUPT NOTE — not raw tool output), then your text. Leave empty for a plain stop.
mcpDetailModal.abortNotePlaceholder: e.g. Output is enough—skip waiting and continue…
mcpDetailModal.execSuccessNoContent: Execution succeeded with no displayable content.
mcpDetailModal.contentTruncated: … (display truncated; use read_file on the path in persisted-output for the full file)

externalMcpModal.formatDesc: JSON object; key = config name, value = config. Use Start/Stop buttons to control state. Supports ${VAR} and ${VAR:-default} env variable syntax.

skillModal.skillNameHint: Lowercase letters, digits, hyphens (Agent Skills name)
skillModal.descriptionHint: Maps to the description field in SKILL.md YAML (when creating/editing SKILL.md)
skillModal.packageFilesHint: Click a file to edit; folders are labels only and cannot be opened
skillModal.newFilePlaceholder: Relative path, e.g. FORMS.md or scripts/extra.sh
skillModal.newFilePathRequired: Enter a path for the new file
skillModal.unsavedSwitch: You have unsaved changes. Switch file anyway?
skillModal.contentPlaceholderAdd: SKILL.md body only (front matter is generated)…
skillModal.bodyHintEdit: For SKILL.md this is the body only (no --- header); save merges with name/description into standard SKILL.md.
skillModal.contentHintAdd: Creates standard SKILL.md (YAML front matter + body). Drop open-source skill folders into skills/ as-is.

batchManageModal.title: Manage conversations · {{count}} total
batchManageModal.confirmDeleteNone: Please select at least one conversation to delete
batchManageModal.confirmDeleteN: Delete {{count}} selected conversation(s)? Chat messages cannot be recovered, but recorded vulnerabilities will remain in the vulnerability library.
batchManageModal.confirmGroupChangeNone: Please select at least one conversation
batchManageModal.confirmRemoveNoGroup: None of the selected conversations belong to a group
batchManageModal.confirmRemoveN: Remove {{count}} selected conversation(s) from their group(s)?

createGroupModal.title: Create group
createGroupModal.nameExists: Group name already exists, please use another name.

batchImportModal.title: New task
batchImportModal.queueTitlePlaceholder: Enter queue title (optional, for identification and filtering)
batchImportModal.queueTitleHint: Set a title for the batch task queue to make it easier to find and manage later.
batchImportModal.roleHint: Select a role; all tasks will be executed using that role's configuration (prompt and tools).
batchImportModal.projectHint: Optionally bind this queue to a project; leave empty to keep it unbound.
batchImportModal.agentModeHint: Same as chat: Eino single-agent (ADK), or Deep / Plan-Execute / Supervisor (last three require multi_agent.enabled).
batchImportModal.concurrencyHint: Number of subtasks to run in parallel (1-8). Default 1 is serial; use 1-2 for scan-heavy tasks.
batchImportModal.scheduleModeHint: Manual is for one-time runs; Cron is for recurring runs. Validate tasks manually first.
batchImportModal.cronExprHint: Use standard 5-field Cron: minute hour day month weekday. Example: `0 2 * * *` runs at 02:00 daily.
batchImportModal.cronExprRequired: Please fill in a Cron expression when Cron schedule is selected
batchImportModal.cronExprInvalid: Invalid Cron expression format. Must have 5 fields (minute hour day month weekday), e.g.: 0 */2 * * *
batchImportModal.executeNowHint: Default is off. When disabled, the queue stays pending and can be started manually later.
batchImportModal.tasksListPlaceholder: Enter task list, one per line
batchImportModal.tasksListPlaceholderExample:
Enter task list, one per line, for example:
Scan open ports of 192.168.1.1
Check if https://example.com has SQL injection
Enumerate subdomains of example.com
batchImportModal.tasksListHint: Enter one task command per line; the system runs them via a concurrency pool. Empty lines are ignored.
batchImportModal.tasksListHintFull: Hint: Enter one task command per line; the system runs them via a concurrency pool. Empty lines are ignored.

batchQueueDetailModal.title: Batch queue details
batchQueueDetailModal.startExecuteNowConfirm: This is a Cron queue. Clicking Start will run the current round immediately instead of waiting for the next Cron time. Continue?
batchQueueDetailModal.scheduleCronAutoHint: When off, the cron expression is kept but the queue will not run on schedule; use Start to run manually.
batchQueueDetailModal.concurrencyEditHint: Click to edit. Cannot change while the queue is running.

vulnerabilityModal.projectHint: Bound findings appear in list_vulnerabilities for that project; leave empty to infer from the conversation when possible.
vulnerabilityModal.title: Title
vulnerabilityModal.descriptionPlaceholder: Describe the summary, trigger point, observed abnormal behavior, and why it is exploitable.
vulnerabilityModal.targetPlaceholder: Be specific: URL, IP:port, endpoint path, and parameter name.
vulnerabilityModal.preconditionsPlaceholder: Login state, permissions, account, headers/cookies, required data, environment/version; write none if not needed.
vulnerabilityModal.reproductionStepsPlaceholder: Number the steps and include entry point, parameter, payload, command, and observation point.
vulnerabilityModal.evidencePlaceholder: Raw request/response, curl/tool command, screenshot notes, logs, DNSLog/callback records, database results, file paths, timestamps, etc.
vulnerabilityModal.impactPlaceholder: Describe the verified real-world impact, such as which data can be read or changed.
vulnerabilityModal.recommendationPlaceholder: Write the concrete fix and retest criteria.
vulnerabilityModal.retestNotesPlaceholder: How to verify the fix, including expected status code, error message, or access-control result.

roleModal.userPromptHint: This prompt is appended before user message to guide AI. It does not change system prompt.
roleModal.defaultRoleToolsDesc: Default role uses all tools enabled in MCP Management.
roleModal.relatedToolsHint: Use “Linked / Not linked” above to filter by this role’s checkboxes. MCP-wide on/off is in MCP Management.
roleModal.firstRoleNoToolsHint: First role with no tools selected will use all tools by default.
roleModal.statsPageLinkedTitle: Checked = link tool to this role; unrelated to MCP on/off
roleModal.statsRoleLinkedTitle: Numerator: checked tools (MCP on only). Denominator: MCP-on tool count (same as MCP Management filter)
roleModal.statsRoleLinkedNoMax: Role checked: {{current}} (switch filter to All, no search, load once to sync cap)
roleModal.statsRoleUsesAll: Policy: all MCP-on tools ({{mcpOn}}) · {{all}} total in catalog (incl. MCP off)
roleModal.statsRoleUsesAllTitle: Matches MCP Management “enabled” count; no explicit tool list
roleModal.statsListScopeRoleOff: List: not linked to this role {{n}}
roleModal.mcpDisabledBadgeTitle: Off in MCP Management; check only expresses role linkage—turn on in MCP to run
roleModal.roleFilterOnBanner: These tools are checked and linked to this role (independent of MCP-wide enable).
roleModal.roleFilterOffBanner: These tools are unchecked and not linked to this role.
roleModal.checkboxLinkTitle: Check to link this tool to this role
roleModal.bindWorkflowHint: When a workflow is selected, conversations with this role automatically run the bound graph; workflow fields are configured freely in the graph JSON.

workflows.title: Graph Orchestration
workflows.metaIdHint: Cannot be changed after creation; used for API and role binding
workflows.metaEnabledHint: Disabled workflows cannot be auto-triggered by roles
workflows.canvasEmpty: Drag nodes from the left onto the canvas, or click node buttons to add quickly
workflows.propertyEmpty: Select a node or edge to edit properties

config.inputBindingHint: from = data source, field = field name (e.g. output, message)
config.instructionPlaceholder: Describe what this node should accomplish
config.conditionHint: The node computes matched (true/false); outgoing edges define branches: first edge is "Yes", second is "No". You can also write {{previous.matched}} == "true" on the edge.
config.edgeBranchHint: The first edge from a condition node defaults to the "Yes" branch, the second to "No"; you can customize conditions here.
config.joinStrategyHint: When multiple upstream nodes enter this node, choose how to build previous: merge all, last by canvas order, first non-empty, or fail fast.
config.hitlInteractiveHint: The run pauses at this node; approve or reject via API or the monitor panel to continue.
config.promptBindingHint: When prompt text is empty, read approval text from the bound field
config.sourceBindingHint: Write the bound field value to the output variable; static value below overrides when set
config.resultBindingHint: Field shown in the end node summary

workflows.defaultHitlPrompt: Please approve whether this step should continue
workflows.idNameRequired: Workflow ID and name are required
workflows.duplicateEdge: An edge already exists between these two nodes
workflows.connectModeOn: Connect mode: click source node then target node

validation.needStart: At least one Start node is required
validation.needOutput: At least one Output node is required
validation.edgeSourceMissing: Edge {{id}} source node does not exist
validation.edgeTargetMissing: Edge {{id}} target node does not exist
validation.startIncoming: Start node {{label}} must not have incoming edges
validation.outputOutgoing: Output node {{label}} must not have outgoing edges
validation.toolNeedsMcp: Tool node {{label}} requires an MCP tool
validation.conditionNeedsExpr: Condition node {{label}} requires a condition expression
validation.conditionNeedsOutEdge: Condition node {{label}} needs at least one outgoing edge (Yes/No branch)
validation.conditionTooManyEdges: Condition node {{label}} should have at most two outgoing edges (Yes/No); configure edge conditions for a third and beyond
validation.conditionBranchLabel: Condition node {{label}} outgoing edges must be marked Yes/No
validation.nodeUnreachable: Node {{label}} cannot be reached from Start
validation.graphCycle: Workflow contains a cycle; keep it as a DAG
validation.outputNeedsKey: Output node {{label}} requires an output variable name

files.uploadUnsupported: Upload is not supported for this session
files.uploadCurlBeacon: Curl beacons cannot upload files; use an HTTP Beacon
files.uploadTcpShell: This is a TCP reverse shell (bash/nc): commands and download only. For upload, reconnect with: (1) a compiled CSB1 Beacon on the same listener, or (2) an HTTP/HTTPS Beacon.
files.uploadTcpReverse: This is a TCP reverse shell (bash/nc): commands and download only. For upload, reconnect with: (1) a compiled CSB1 Beacon on the same listener, or (2) an HTTP/HTTPS Beacon.

listeners.title: Listener Management
listeners.name: Name
listeners.emptyHint: Create your first C2 listener using the button below
listeners.callbackHostHint: Public IP or hostname stored for payloads/beacons; separate from bind address. If empty, payload generation falls back to bind address / auto-detect.
listeners.allowLegacyShell: Allow unencrypted classic reverse shell (lab only)
listeners.allowLegacyShellHint: Off by default. When enabled, raw bash/nc TCP connections register sessions and are vulnerable to internet scanners; use encrypted Beacon builds for production.
listeners.malleableProfileHint: Optional; HTTP/HTTPS Beacon response headers and traffic disguise. Stop and start the listener again for changes to take effect.
listeners.malleableProfileNonHttpHint: This listener type does not use a Malleable Profile. You can still bind one here for later if you switch to HTTP/HTTPS Beacon.
listeners.malleableProfileEmptyListHint: No saved profiles yet. Create one under C2 → Traffic disguise / Malleable Profile, then pick it here.
listeners.confirmDelete: Delete this listener? All related sessions and tasks will be removed.
listeners.toastFillRequired: Please fill in all required fields

sessions.title: Session Management
sessions.emptyHint: Start a listener and run a payload on the target
sessions.curlBeaconBody:
This session was created with a Curl oneliner: heartbeat and tasks only.
Interactive terminal and file management require a compiled Beacon binary or a TCP reverse listener.
sessions.sleepModalHint: Saves to the server and queues a sleep task. The implant applies it on the next task poll; later check-ins keep this config.
sessions.toastSleepInvalid: Sleep interval must be at least 1 second
sessions.confirmExitSession: Send exit command to this session?
sessions.confirmDeleteSession: Remove this session and related tasks/files from the server? (Does not send exit to the implant; use Kill Session to exit the agent.)
sessions.terminalWelcome: CyberStrikeAI C2 Terminal — Enter to run; ↑↓ history; Ctrl+L clear; Ctrl+C cancel input
sessions.termWaitFinish: Please wait for the current command to finish
sessions.termCtrlC: Remote interrupt is not supported in this version
sessions.termQueued: [Command queued — will run after the current task completes]
sessions.emptyFilter: No sessions match the current filters
sessions.selectPromptHint: Click a session in the list on the left to view terminal, files, and tasks.
sessions.confirmBatchDelete: Delete {{n}} selected session(s)? Related tasks and file records will be removed.
sessions.confirmDeleteFiltered: Delete all {{n}} session(s) in the current filter results?
sessions.toastSelectFirst: Select at least one session to delete

tasks.title: Task Management
tasks.confirmDeleteOne: Delete this task record from the server? This cannot be undone.
tasks.confirmBatchDelete: Delete {{n}} selected task record(s)? This cannot be undone.

payloads.title: Payload Generator
payloads.onelinerDesc: Generate a one-line reverse shell for the target (Bash / Python / PowerShell / Curl).
payloads.buildDesc: Cross-compile a full Beacon binary for Linux / Windows / macOS.
payloads.placeholderListenerHost: Leave empty: listener callback host, else bind address
payloads.loopbackBeaconWarning: 127.0.0.1 works only when the beacon and C2 share the same host and network namespace (simple local test). If C2 runs in Docker and the beacon runs on the host, 127.0.0.1 hits the host loopback, not the container — use the published port plus the host LAN IP or host.docker.internal. For remote targets, bind 0.0.0.0 and rebuild.
payloads.noBeaconListenersTcpOnly: tcp_reverse supports compiled Beacon (TCP + CSB1 framing); classic shell still uses Oneliner above

events.title: Event Audit
events.confirmDeleteOne: Delete this event? This cannot be undone.
events.confirmBatchDelete: Delete {{n}} selected event(s)? This cannot be undone.

profiles.title: Malleable Profile
profiles.name: Name
profiles.hintUa: Custom User-Agent for Beacon HTTP requests
profiles.hintUris: URI paths used when the beacon checks in
profiles.hintHeaders: HTTP response headers to mimic a legitimate server

rbac.title: Platform Access
rbac.pageSubtitle: Manage members, roles, and accessible data in one place.
rbac.memberManagementHint: Assign roles and data scope to members
rbac.roleManagementHint: Define permission sets and default visibility
rbac.usersHint: Select an account to manage roles and resource scope.
rbac.selectUserHint: Select a platform user to edit roles, status, and resource grants.
rbac.roleMembershipHint: Roles control allowed actions; resource scope controls visible data.
rbac.resourceIdPlaceholder: Enter resource IDs; separate multiple IDs with commas
rbac.advancedAssignmentHint: For known IDs copied from logs or APIs. Resource existence is still validated on submit.
rbac.availableResourcesHint: Select on this page; selections persist across pages
rbac.searchAssignments: Search granted resource names or IDs
rbac.assignmentHelp: Only Assigned Resources roles need explicit grants. Project grants also cover conversations and vulnerabilities in that project.
rbac.roleCatalogHint: Define permissions and resource scope first, then assign the role to members.
rbac.passwordPlaceholder: Leave blank when editing to keep unchanged
rbac.deleteUserConfirm: Delete this platform user? This will revoke the user's active sessions immediately.
rbac.deleteBuiltinUser: The built-in admin user cannot be deleted
rbac.scopeHint: Resource scope and operation permissions jointly determine effective access.
rbac.confirmDiscardSwitchUser: Role changes are not saved. Discard them and switch members?
rbac.confirmDiscardEditUser: Role changes are not saved. Discard them and edit the member profile?
rbac.confirmDiscardSwitchTab: Role changes are not saved. Discard them and switch tabs?
rbac.confirmDiscardResourceType: Switching resource type clears the current selection. Continue?
rbac.noRoleNoticeHint: Assign a role before configuring resource grants. Without a role, only self-created data is accessible and most features are unavailable.
rbac.editUserRolesHint: Manage roles on the member detail page under Roles & Permissions.
rbac.auditHint: Audit events related to this member's platform access.
rbac.deleteUserConfirmDetailed:
Delete member {{name}} (@{{username}})?

This will revoke active sessions and remove {{roles}} role bindings and {{grants}} resource grants.

all.label: All Resources
all.option: All Resources — Access all platform data

assigned.label: Assigned Resources
assigned.hint: Only data explicitly granted by an administrator

own.label: Created by Me
own.hint: Only data created by the member
own.option: Created by Me — Only self-created data

operator.name: Operator
operator.description: Run daily security workflows without account or core configuration management

auditor.name: Auditor
auditor.description: Read-only access to audits, monitoring, and assets

viewer.name: Read-only User
viewer.description: Read-only access to explicitly granted resources

empty.addFirstUser: Use “Add Member” to create the first member
empty.adjustRoleSearch: Adjust the search or create a custom role
empty.addAssignmentHint: Select resources on the left, then use Confirm Grant above
empty.adjustAssignmentFilter: Adjust the resource type or search
empty.assignRoleFirst: Assign a role to view permission details
empty.noAuditLogsHint: Role and grant changes for this member will appear here

messages.roleChangesSaved: Role changes saved and effective immediately
messages.grantPartial: Added {{created}} grants; {{skipped}} already existed or were duplicated

errors.enterResourceId: Enter at least one resource ID
